name: Macao (China)

colors.symbolism: the territory and a new beginning
the lotus flower, the bridge and the water
the five stars representing the People's Republic of China

description: A green field bearing, in the centre, a white lotus with three blossoms above a stylised white bridge and white water, surmounted by an arc of five yellow five-pointed stars — one large star with four smaller stars curving above it.
history: Adopted on 31 March 1993 by the Preparatory Committee for the Macao Special Administrative Region and hoisted on 20 December 1999, when Portugal transferred sovereignty over Macao to the People's Republic of China. The five-star arc directly echoes the flag of the PRC.

features.symbolism: green represents the lush flora of the territory and is also associated with hope and a new beginning under Chinese sovereignty
the lotus, traditional emblem of Macao; the three blossoms represent the Macao Peninsula and the islands of Taipa and Coloane
the bridge linking the Macao Peninsula and Taipa, depicted in stylised form beneath the lotus
the sea, on which Macao depends and which surrounds the territory

features.role: large central star above the lotus
four smaller stars arrayed around the large star
features.symbolism: echoing the five stars of the flag of the People's Republic of China, signalling Macao's status as a Special Administrative Region
